context: Tests whether the agent correctly documents the error recovery workflow: checking terminal output, inspecting the screenshots/ directory for auto-captured error screenshots, re-running npm run sync to start fresh, and verifying success via Reclaim Settings > Hours.

checklist.name: Check terminal output
checklist.description: troubleshooting-guide.md or failure-checklist.md instructs checking terminal output to identify which timezone failed and why

checklist.name: Screenshots directory
checklist.description: At least one output file explicitly mentions checking the 'screenshots/' directory for automatically captured error screenshots

checklist.name: Re-run to recover
checklist.description: At least one output file instructs re-running 'npm run sync' as the recovery step (not a partial fix or manual edit)

checklist.name: Fresh start on re-run
checklist.description: At least one output file explains that re-running npm run sync starts fresh — it deletes remaining entries and re-adds all of them

checklist.name: Destructive operation awareness
checklist.description: At least one output file acknowledges that the sync is destructive — it deletes ALL existing Reclaim travel timezone entries before adding new ones

checklist.name: Verify in Reclaim UI
checklist.description: troubleshooting-guide.md includes a verification step that checks Reclaim Settings > Hours to confirm all entries were added correctly

checklist.name: Correct recovery command path
checklist.description: The recovery command in any output file uses the correct project path /Users/jbaruch/Projects/reclaim-tripit-timezones-sync when showing the npm run sync command
